original_description: Explanation engine of the Alexandria learning suite. Use ONLY when the user explicitly invokes Alexandria by name or with /alexandria-teach to have code taught to them as a lesson (e.g. "alexandria, teach me this file"). Never use for ordinary coding, debugging, or code-review requests that don't name Alexandria.

train_results.query: alexandria, teach me how the OrderRepository in app/code/Basys/IQProPayment/Model/OrderRepository.php works, intro level please
hey alexandria can you teach me what this webhook verification code in src/Webhooks/Verifier.php actually does? i'm new to hmac stuff
use /alexandria-teach on src/hooks/useCheckout.ts — i want the practitioner version this time
alexandria teach: the replication code in Source/Multiplayer/CharacterSync.cpp, deep-dive depth
I just inherited this Magento plugin from a contractor who left no docs. alexandria, walk me through etc/di.xml as a lesson — assume I know nothing about dependency injection
can alexandria explain the auth middleware to me like i'm brand new? file is packages/api/src/middleware/auth.guard.ts
ok time for another alexandria lesson. today: how does scripts/vault_lint.py decide a wiki-link is dead?
My boss wants me to understand the payment tokenization flow before the Friday demo. Alexandria, teach me src/tokenizer.js at intro depth
alexandria, i want a lesson on the recursion in utils/tree_flatten.py — and quiz me after
start an alexandria teaching session on how the GraphQL resolvers work in this repo, resolvers/order.resolver.ts specifically
fix the null pointer crash in src/checkout/session.php when the cart is empty
explain this file to me like I'm new to PHP — src/Gateway/Client.php
teach me how async/await works in javascript, quick examples are fine
can you walk me through what happens when a user checks out? just a quick explanation, nothing fancy
write docstrings for every function in scripts/vault.py
add a section to README.md describing what the alexandria-teach skill does
review my PR diff and tell me if the error handling in the webhook route is right
i don't get why this regex in validate.ts keeps failing on unicode input, debug it for me
alexandria, file this lesson away in the vault please — concepts were HMAC verification and replay protection
create an onboarding doc for new engineers explaining our repo structure and conventions
